turns.svg:
<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 512 512' width='512' height='512'>
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id='bgGrad' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0' stop-color='#f7fbff'/>
      <stop offset='1' stop-color='#e6f2ff'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id='coinGrad' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0' stop-color='#ffe48c'/>
      <stop offset='1' stop-color='#d99e1a'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id='barGrad' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0' stop-color='#7bd389'/>
      <stop offset='1' stop-color='#2ea45a'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id='softShadow' x='-50%' y='-50%' width='200%' height='200%'>
      <feGaussianBlur in='SourceAlpha' stdDeviation='6' result='blur'/>
      <feOffset in='blur' dx='0' dy='6' result='off'/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in='off'/>
        <feMergeNode in='SourceGraphic'/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect x='0' y='0' width='512' height='512' fill='url(#bgGrad)' />

  <!-- coins stack -->
  <g transform='translate(100,0)' filter='url(#softShadow)'>
    <!-- shadow ellipse under stack -->
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='445' rx='70' ry='16' fill='rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' />

    <!-- coins (ellipses stacked) -->
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='420' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='400' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='380' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='362' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='346' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>

    <!-- top highlights -->
    <ellipse cx='-10' cy='338' rx='22' ry='6' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.45)' />
    <ellipse cx='-8' cy='358' rx='18' ry='4' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.25)' />
  </g>

  <!-- ascending bars (bar chart) -->
  <g transform='translate(0,0)'>
    <!-- baseline -->
    <rect x='180' y='450' width='180' height='6' rx='3' fill='#dfeff0' />

    <!-- bars -->
    <rect x='200' y='360' width='48' height='90' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' />
    <rect x='268' y='300' width='48' height='150' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' opacity='0.95' />
    <rect x='336' y='220' width='48' height='230' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' opacity='0.9' />
    <rect x='404' y='150' width='48' height='300' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' opacity='0.88' />
  </g>

  <!-- upward arrow indicating growth -->
  <g stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'>
    <path d='M120 430 C180 360, 260 300, 430 120' fill='none' stroke='#ff9f2e' stroke-width='18' stroke-opacity='0.95' />
    <!-- arrow head -->
    <polygon points='430,120 408,140 444,142' fill='#ff9f2e' stroke='#d46f00' stroke-width='2'/>
    <!-- subtle highlight on arrow -->
    <path d='M132 418 C188 360, 270 308, 420 140' fill='none' stroke='rgba(255,255,255,0.18)' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round' />
  </g>

  <!-- small coin near bars to suggest investment allocation -->
  <g transform='translate(320,360)'>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='0' rx='28' ry='8' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='-8' cy='-8' rx='12' ry='4' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.35)' />
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 512 512' width='512' height='512'>
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id='bgGrad' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0' stop-color='#f7fbff'/>
      <stop offset='1' stop-color='#e6f2ff'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id='coinGrad' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0' stop-color='#ffe48c'/>
      <stop offset='1' stop-color='#d99e1a'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id='barGrad' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0' stop-color='#7bd389'/>
      <stop offset='1' stop-color='#2ea45a'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id='softShadow' x='-50%' y='-50%' width='200%' height='200%'>
      <feGaussianBlur in='SourceAlpha' stdDeviation='6' result='blur'/>
      <feOffset in='blur' dx='0' dy='6' result='off'/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in='off'/>
        <feMergeNode in='SourceGraphic'/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect x='0' y='0' width='512' height='512' fill='url(#bgGrad)' />

  <!-- coins stack -->
  <g transform='translate(100,0)' filter='url(#softShadow)'>
    <!-- shadow ellipse under stack -->
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='445' rx='70' ry='16' fill='rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' />

    <!-- coins (ellipses stacked) -->
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='420' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='400' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='380' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='362' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='346' rx='48' ry='14' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>

    <!-- top highlights -->
    <ellipse cx='-10' cy='338' rx='22' ry='6' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.45)' />
    <ellipse cx='-8' cy='358' rx='18' ry='4' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.25)' />
  </g>

  <!-- ascending bars (bar chart) -->
  <g transform='translate(0,0)'>
    <!-- baseline -->
    <rect x='180' y='450' width='180' height='6' rx='3' fill='#dfeff0' />

    <!-- bars -->
    <rect x='200' y='360' width='48' height='90' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' />
    <rect x='268' y='300' width='48' height='150' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' opacity='0.95' />
    <rect x='336' y='220' width='48' height='230' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' opacity='0.9' />
    <rect x='404' y='150' width='48' height='300' rx='6' fill='url(#barGrad)' opacity='0.88' />
  </g>

  <!-- upward arrow indicating growth -->
  <g stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'>
    <path d='M120 430 C180 360, 260 300, 430 120' fill='none' stroke='#ff9f2e' stroke-width='18' stroke-opacity='0.95' />
    <!-- arrow head -->
    <polygon points='430,120 408,140 444,142' fill='#ff9f2e' stroke='#d46f00' stroke-width='2'/>
    <!-- subtle highlight on arrow -->
    <path d='M132 418 C188 360, 270 308, 420 140' fill='none' stroke='rgba(255,255,255,0.18)' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round' />
  </g>

  <!-- small coin near bars to suggest investment allocation -->
  <g transform='translate(320,360)'>
    <ellipse cx='0' cy='0' rx='28' ry='8' fill='url(#coinGrad)' stroke='#b27d12' stroke-width='2'/>
    <ellipse cx='-8' cy='-8' rx='12' ry='4' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.35)' />
  </g>

</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG Elements**

I need to generate JSON with a single field "svg" that includes a full <svg> string without any text or numbers. The concept is to visually represent the word "investment" using symbols like a pile of coins, an upward arrow, a bar chart, and a piggy bank. I’ll use basic shapes, ensuring simplicity, with stacks of coins depicted as circles. The overall design will include a bar chart with ascending bars and a valid SVG structure. Colors will be incorporated, and everything must be valid and straightforward.**Designing SVG Layout**

I'm positioning the elements at the bottom center left. I'll have a thick upward arrow in gold or orange pointing upwards over the bars. The coins will be represented as a stack of golden circles with an ellipse shadowing for a 3D effect at the base of the bars on the left side. I’ll keep the design simple, avoiding any foreignObjects or external styles. The JSON string will need proper escaping for the SVG, using double quotes for property names and values, and I’ll keep everything valid and straightforward.**Simplifying SVG Syntax**

To make it easier, I'll use single quotes for the SVG attributes, so I won't need to escape the quotes except for the wrapping JSON. I can structure the JSON like this: 

{ "svg": "<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 512 512' width='512' height='512'> ... </svg>" }.

I'll keep things compact without any unnecessary newlines. For example, I'll create a gradient in the definitions section using single quotes. The background will feature a gradient rectangle, and for the coins, I'll position a group around (80, 360) and create four stacked circles. I'll include shading and highlights with ellipses and a darker stroke.**Constructing Bar Chart**

I'm designing a bar chart with four rectangles positioned at x coordinates 220, 280, 340, and 400. Their widths will be 40, and the heights will be ascending at 120, 180, 260, and 320 pixels, with the y positions calculated by subtracting the height from the canvas height of 512, leaving a bottom margin of about 60. For rounded rectangles, I'll use a corner radius. There's also an upward arrow from the coins to above the bars, which will be set in orange. I need to finalize the exact coordinates and ensure they're within bounds.**Finalizing SVG Structure**

I need to adjust the x positions for the bars to 200, 260, 320, and 380, ensuring the last bar fits comfortably within the canvas width. The coins will be centered around x=80, and I'll stack five coins using ellipses with specific radius and vertical offsets for a nice staggered appearance. The colors will be gold using gradients or specific codes. The arrow will extend from near (110, 430) to (430, 110) with rounded ends. I’ll keep it simple without too many shapes or text labels, and I’ll produce a JSON string while validating with single quotes and managing newlines for readability.

I’ll keep it simple without too many shapes or text labels, and I’ll produce a JSON string while validating with single quotes and managing newlines for readability.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
